data.title: Kinross suspends Tasiast mill operations due to fire
data.description:
Kinross Gold (NYSE:KGC) -1.1% pre-market Tuesday after saying it temporarily suspended mill operations at its Tasiast mine in Mauritania due to a fire that occurred on April 14, which has been fully extinguished.

Kinross (NYSE:KGC) said it is investigating the cause of the fire and assessing the potential impact on the operation, but its initial assessment indicates the fire was localized in the SAG discharge area.

The company said critical spare parts are available on site, and it is not expecting to change its guidance for Tasiast.

Tasiast produced a record 622,394 gold equivalent oz in FY 2024, which the company said was mainly the result of record throughput following the completion of the Tasiast 24K project in H2 20 2023.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Tuesday: JNJ, BAC, C and UAL
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Tuesday seesawed, as market participants digested some major quarterly results and kept a close eye on tariff developments. Here are some stocks to watch on Tuesday: 

  • Shares of Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) were -0.4% in pre-market trading. The pharmaceutical giant delivered a solid Q1 top- and bottom-line beat. It also raised its full-year operational sales guidance to reflect the addition of bipolar depression drug Caplyta following the closing of its $14.6B acquisition of Intra-Cellular Therapies. J&J (NYSE:JNJ) maintained its full-year adjusted profit per share growth outlook, which includes tariff costs and dilution from the acquisition. See how JNJ performed in charts.
  • Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) stock was +2.5% ahead of the opening bell. The number two U.S. bank by assets saw a 10.8% Y/Y rise in Q1 net income. "Our business clients have been performing well; and consumers have shown resilience, continuing to spend and maintaining healthy credit quality," chief executive Brian Moynihan said. “We run our business in a manner intended to withstand volatility for the long-term," finance chief Alastair Borthwick added. See how BAC performed in charts.          
  • Shares of Citi (NYSE:C) were +1.6% ahead of market open. The number four U.S. bank reported a 21% Y/Y jump in Q1 net income. The quarter was "marked by continued momentum, positive operating leverage" and improved returns in Citi's (NYSE:C) businesses, according to top boss Jane Fraser. "When all is said and done, and longstanding trade imbalances and other structural shifts are behind us, the U.S. will still be the world’s leading economy," she added. See how C performed in charts.
  • United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) stock will be in focus, ahead of the carrier's Q1 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects UAL to earn 74 cents per share on revenue of $13.18B. In the same quarter a year ago, United (NASDAQ:UAL) had delivered a loss due to a $200M impact from the grounding of Boeing's (BA) 737 Max 9 jets. UAL achieved record Q4 2024 profit, helped by strong demand from premium customers, corporates and basic economy travelers.

data.title: Walker & Dunlop secures $237 million refinance for premier mixed-use community
data.description:
• Walker & Dunlop (NYSE:WD) announced on Tuesday that it arranged $237.5 million in financing for Edge-on-Hudson, a transit-oriented mixed-use community spanning 70 acres, and the Daymark, a premier 5-story luxury condominium located within the Edge-on-Hudson development along the Hudson River, in Sleepy Hollow, New York.
  • The Walker & Dunlop New York Capital Markets team arranged the refinancing package as exclusive advisors to King Street Capital Management and Biddle Real Estate Ventures (BREV), with Hudson Bay Capital providing the funding.
  • Shares of WD were up +0.49% to $75.54 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nomura sells Tokyo training center to real estate affiliate - report
data.description:
Nomura Holdings (NYSE:NMR) offloaded its training center in Tokyo’s Minato district to Nomura Real Estate Holdings Inc. (OTCPK:NMEHF), its real estate affiliate, according to a media report dated Tuesday.

The purchaser intends to demolish the building and construct high-end condominiums on the property, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Nomura (NYSE:NMR) said in February notice that it would sell the training center,

It expects to record a ¥56B ($392M) gain from the divestment in the quarter ending June 30, Bloomberg reported.

NMR shares edged up 1.1% in Tuesday premarket trading in the U.S.

data.description:
• Sienna Senior Living Inc. (TSX:SIA:CA) declares CAD 0.078/share monthly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record April 30; ex-div April 30.

data.title: ASP Isotopes appoint Maseko to board of directors, Strydom to board of managers
data.description:
• ASP Isotopes (NASDAQ:ASPI) has announced that Mr. Sipho Maseko has joined the board of directors of ASP Isotopes.
  • Dr. Hendrik Strydom, PhD, CTO of ASP Isotopes, has transitioned from the board of directors of ASP Isotopes to the board of managers of Quantum Leap Energy, the Co.’s subsidiary that is pursuing an initiative to produce advanced nuclear fuels, such as HALEU and Lithium-6, in South Africa.
  • The Co. intends to pursue the separation of its nuclear fuels business, Quantum Leap Energy, and specialist isotope products and related services business in two independent companies during 2H 2025.

data.title: Optellum partners with Bristol Myers to use AI-driven lung cancer diagnosis
data.description:
• Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) has signed an agreement with Optellum, an AI-driven clinical decision support company for early lung cancer diagnosis.
  • Building on an existing research collaboration, Optellum will be deploying its Virtual Nodule Clinic platform, an AI-powered decision support tool for early-stage lung cancer, at multiple healthcare systems across the United States.
  • "The agreement with Bristol Myers Squibb is a key step that helps Optellum accelerate AI-powered early diagnosis, from top academic medical centers to community centers caring for underserved patient populations who can benefit the most,"  said Václav Potěšil, Optellum Founder and Chief Business Officer.

data.title: Retailers: Seeking Alpha Analysts vs. Wall Street
data.description:
The retail sector has taken quite some beating on account of the uncertainty plagued by the tariff-related announcements. However, the recent 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for all countries, except China, has helped the sector get some respite. 

Interestingly, retail sales grew moderately in March after two straight months of declines as consumers were stocking up to get ahead of tariffs.

"With the economic outlook unclear and the situation fluid, consumer sentiment is weakening, and many consumers are shifting disposable income into savings," noted an industry body CEO. 

In such a prevailing atmosphere, Wall Street analysts have appeared to be more favorable toward these stocks than Seeking Alpha analysts.

Looking at the chart above, Seeking Alpha analysts appeared cautious on Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST), Home Depot (NYSE:HD), and TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), while the sell side gave them a bullish mandate. The opposite was the case for Target (NYSE:TGT) which was rated favorably by SA compared to Wall Street. 

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Alibaba Group (NYSE:BABA) were rated mostly in green by both sides. While Kroger (NYSE:KR) and Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW) received a mix of opinions from the analysts.

data.title: Archer-Daniels Midland upgraded at Bank of America on better risk profile
data.description:
Archer-Daniels-Midland (NYSE:ADM) on Tuesday was upgraded to Neutral from Underperform by analysts at Bank of America. They said recent weakness in shares of the food processing and commodities trading company put its stock price in line with its risk-reward profile.

Despite ongoing earnings challenges, ADM (NYSE:ADM) is seen as better positioned than many peers amid the current macroeconomic and trade environment, Salvator Tiano, research analyst at Bank of America, said in an April 15 note to clients.

ADM (ADM) is poised to benefit from newly imposed U.S. tariffs, particularly on imported feedstocks, which are expected to support domestic soybean crush margins and biodiesel profitability. However, Bank of America continues to see headwinds in other parts of ADM’s (ADM) business, especially agricultural trading and energy-linked segments like ethanol and soybean oil.

Chinese tariffs on U.S. crop exports are already creating pressure on ADM’s (ADM) trading operations. The shift in global trade flows toward Latin America — which has less exposure to such tariffs — is seen as a relative disadvantage for ADM (ADM), given its stronger U.S.-based footprint. Additionally, lower global energy prices are expected to weigh on biodiesel and ethanol margins, contributing to a more challenging earnings landscape.

Bank of America cut its 2025 earnings per share forecast to $4.00 from $4.10, near the low end of ADM’s (ADM) guidance range of $4.00–$4.75. The first-quarter EPS estimate was trimmed by one cent to $0.69. For 2026, the firm continues to expect a meaningful earnings rebound, though it lowered its estimate to $5.00 from $5.30, assuming a recovery in crush margins and potential insurance proceeds.

Despite the upgrade, Bank of America reiterated a preference for ADM (ADM) rival Bunge (NYSE:BG), which it views more favorably due to its global footprint, stronger growth potential, and relative valuation.

The revised price objective for ADM is based on a blended average of discounted cash flow and price-to-earnings models, now using a higher weighted average cost of capital of 8.1%, up from 7.9%, reflecting increased market risk and financing costs.

The firm set a new price objective of $47 on ADM (ADM), slightly down from the previous $48, reflecting revised earnings forecasts and a higher cost of equity.

data.title: Prudential Financial to redeem all outstanding subordinated notes due 2045
data.description:
• Prudential Financial (NYSE:PRU) plans to redeem all of the $1B outstanding aggregate principal amount of its 5.375% Fixed-to-Floating Rate Junior Subordinated Notes due 2045.
  • The notes will be redeemed on May 15, 2025 at a price equal to 100% of the principal amount of the Notes, plus accrued and unpaid interest to, but excluding, the redemption date.
